next_steps: Lyme disease serologic testing (EIA followed by Western blot)
Echocardiogram to assess for wall motion abnormalities and valvular function
Cardiac MRI with gadolinium to evaluate for myocardial inflammation or fibrosis
Continuous cardiac telemetry monitoring for progression of heart block
Review of supplement use and discontinuation of potential hepatotoxins
Consultation with cardiology for possible electrophysiology studies

differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune rheumatic disease (e.g., Systemic Lupus Erythematosus)
next_steps: Lyme disease serology (EIA/Western blot) and Q fever serology (Phase I and II antibodies)
Transthoracic echocardiogram to assess for structural heart disease and valvular function
Cardiac MRI with gadolinium to evaluate for myocardial inflammation, edema, or fibrosis
Continuous inpatient cardiac telemetry to monitor for high-grade AV block progression
Comprehensive autoimmune panel including ANA, ENA, and possibly ACE levels
Discontinuation of all herbal and non-essential supplements to address hepatic injury
Full liver function panel and viral hepatitis screening
